title: Build the product shell, inventory, and setup workflow

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows pending

commands.name: Missing setup workflow domain module
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/setup-workflow.test.ts --project property tests/property/setup-workflow.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The focused suites could not import the not-yet-created setup-workflow module

commands.name: Missing resumable setup browser control
commands.expectedFailure: The browser timed out waiting for the not-yet-implemented continue-setup control

commands.name: Shrunk untrusted-summary counterexample
commands.expectedFailure: Fast Check shrank arbitrary model prose to one whitespace character; that exact case is now a permanent example regression

commands.name: Focused examples, properties, component, and HTTP boundary
commands.command: Focused UX-01 unit, property, component, and integration Vitest commands

commands.name: Evidence inventory required UX-01 totals
commands.expectedFailure: The repository inventory still expected seventeen manifests and 172 proof files after UX-01 added the eighteenth manifest and nine unique proof paths

commands.name: Evidence schema, traceability, and inventory reconciliation
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check and pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot

commands.name: Latest-main property matrix exceeded existing test deadlines under parallel load
commands.expectedFailure: DET-04 PROP-10 crossed its five-second deadline and DET-05 PROP-02 crossed its twenty-second deadline while 24 property files competed for the default worker pool; neither invariant produced a counterexample

commands.name: Four property workers still starved the longest generated sequence
commands.expectedFailure: DET-05 PROP-02 reached 25.9 seconds with four property workers, proving that a partial worker cap did not make the unchanged 500-run property deterministic

commands.name: Two complete serial property matrices with unchanged seeds, run counts, and deadlines
Complete security-patched latest-main optimized-production repository verification

results.counterexample: A one-space model summary is preserved as an example regression and cannot affect status provenance

scenarios.scenario: A privacy officer can leave and resume an authorized software setup
A new privacy officer reaches an honestly run-ready configuration
The setup workflow works by keyboard and exposes accessible state names
Empty inventory and a temporary setup error explain how to recover

visualEvidence.rationale: UX-01 changes the primary inventory and setup experience, including sequential blockers, recovery, and responsive layouts. Fifteen source-bound optimized-production screenshots preserve every setup step plus desktop and narrow transition states.

assets.altText: Desktop software inventory with one fictional Northstar record, imported district approval provenance, neutral no-run wording, and a define-authorization next action.
assets.caption: The default inventory names the district status source and next safe action without treating Pactwire as the approver.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup with software and district status selected as complete and authorization identified as the first missing prerequisite.
assets.caption: Step one binds the exact fictional software record to its stored district provenance.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup with authorization selected as needs action and all four later configuration steps explicitly blocked.
assets.caption: No current test authority exists, so the setup directs the user to define tenant, action, and time scope.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup with agreement upload selected and disabled until the earlier authorization step is complete.
assets.caption: The agreement step remains visible while its earlier authorization prerequisite is explained and enforced.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup with human requirement confirmation selected and blocked by the earlier authorization prerequisite.
assets.caption: The screen states that a person must confirm an executable agreement rule, while preserving the sequential blocker.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup with fictional accounts and test fields selected and blocked by the earlier authorization prerequisite.
assets.caption: The test-data step directly requires a fictional account with a test field and cannot bypass earlier setup work.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup with journeys and required visibility selected and blocked by the earlier authorization prerequisite.
assets.caption: The final step requires a runnable named journey with visible checkpoints and preserves the upstream blocker.

assets.altText: Narrow guided setup showing all six steps, authorization as needs action, later steps blocked, and the disabled out-of-order journey action.
assets.caption: The complete blocker chain remains readable and operable without horizontal clipping at the narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Narrow software inventory showing fictional Northstar metadata, imported district status source, neutral test counts, and the continue-setup action.
assets.caption: Inventory provenance, counts, blockers, and the primary action stack cleanly on a narrow screen.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup after recovery with authorization complete, two of six steps complete, and agreement upload promoted to the next safe action.
assets.caption: After current authority is saved, the server-derived workflow advances exactly one prerequisite to agreement upload.

assets.altText: Desktop guided setup with all six prerequisites complete and a run-ready action to queue one named fictional-data test.
assets.caption: Run-ready appears only after the complete persisted prerequisite chain closes with a visible runnable journey.

assets.altText: Desktop software inventory with agreement version one, authorization review date, and queue-a-named-test as the next safe action.
assets.caption: The inventory refreshes from saved configuration and exposes the named-test action without claiming that a test has run.

assets.altText: Narrow guided setup with six of six complete, stored district provenance, run-ready configuration, and the saved journey detail.
assets.caption: The complete deterministic setup state remains readable and actionable at a narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Narrow run-ready inventory with fictional software identity, agreement version, review date, named-test action, and no-run neutral wording.
assets.caption: All inventory facts and the next safe action remain visible without a misleading sampled-run result.

assets.altText: Narrow software setup failure state with direct saved-status-unavailable copy and a visible Try again control.
assets.caption: A temporary setup API failure is labeled as an error, retains the setup shell, and offers a tested retry without claiming it is still loading.

proofs.description: Strict six-step readiness derivation, sequential blockers, stored status provenance, and immutable view output
Visible-step, stored-provenance, complete-readiness, and expired-authorization examples
Fixed-seed status non-interference and complete-prerequisite readiness properties plus the shrunk whitespace regression
Signed HTTP aggregation, persisted authorization refresh, inventory next action, and target-neutral missing record
Accessible named loading state and refresh control rendering
Resume, complete run-ready, keyboard/Axe, and empty/error/retry user workflows
Real Next.js, API, fixture, keyboard, Axe, responsive capture, and source-bound evidence bindings
Read-only aggregation of persisted authorization, agreement, human requirement, persona, and journey facts
Resumable responsive workflow, URL state, progress, blocker, provenance, loading, error, and retry interface
Authenticated, target-neutral, private no-store setup read boundary
Curated red/green record, visual catalog, remaining CI gate, and bounded product claim

knownLimitations: Clean-checkout GitHub Actions verification has not yet run successfully for this source commit, so UX-01 remains IN_PROGRESS.
Run-ready means the saved fictional configuration has every named prerequisite; it is not evidence that a run occurred or that software is safe, compliant, or approved by Pactwire.
The web product uses the controlled fictional fixture; production persistence and deployment are outside this task.
Keyboard and automated WCAG A/AA checks pass, while comprehensive manual screen-reader and WCAG 2.2 review remains QLT-01.
